feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Unexpected Connections Consumption in SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently implementing a Supplier Portal using SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition.

Our current subscription includes:

1,000 Active Users
0 Connections

Authentication is performed through SAP Cloud Identity Services (Identity Authentication - IAS) as our Identity Provider (IdP).

Due to an omission during the commercial contracting process, our subscription was provisioned with Active Users only, while the Connections metric was not included. We are already discussing this situation with our SAP Account Team, but we would like to better understand both the technical behavior and whether anyone has experienced a similar situation.

During our testing, we observed that every external login generates Connections, even though our subscription does not include this metric.

From a technical perspective, we would like to know:

1. Is the generation of the Connections metric mandatory in SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition?
2. Is there any way to completely prevent the generation of the Connections metric through Role Collections, authorizations or SAP Build Work Zone configuration?

From a commercial perspective, we would also appreciate guidance from anyone who has experienced a similar situation.

Any guidance or shared experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Unique employee number based user creation in Identity directory service
entry.content: How to create users based on unique employee number in Identity directory service?

entry.title: AMS: can authorization policies be combined/shared across multiple CAP apps?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm exploring how AMS handles authorization across several independent CAP apps. Each app has its own IAS application and deploys its own DCL base policies. My goal is to understand whether one assignment can cover policies from multiple apps.

What I tried:

  1. Reuse service – From the documentation I couldn't work out how a CAP app is actually linked to a reuse service. Setting consumed-services on the consuming app's IAS instance had no effect: the reuse app didn't appear under "Reuse services consumed by this application", and no reuse dropdown showed in the consuming app's Authorization Policies tab.
  2. Combine Authorization Policies (Add Combination) – Only policies from the same application are selectable.

The docs say a combined policy can "bundle authorization policies within or across solutions":

Questions:

  • Is combining/sharing policies across applications supported for custom CAP apps, or only for SAP-delivered bundled solutions?
  • If supported, what establishes the relationship so another app's base policies become selectable?
  • If not, is the intended pattern to centralize assignment via Identity Directory groups (SCIM2/IPS)?

entry.title: BTP Trail account: Cloud identity services Access denier error
entry.content:
Hi Team,

While accessing the Cloud identity services from BTP Trail account getting 

"You entered a valid URL, but you are not authorized to view the content; contact your system administrator"

Getting this error for quite some time, was there any issue in CIS?

Kindly assist..

Thanks in advance.

Regards,

Venkat

entry.title: Story Report Query
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am trying to create a Story report of talent information or talent flags fields (potential, risk of loss, retention risk, mobility) etc . I am unable to find the schema for the same. Could you please suggest the schema.Also let me know if we cannot create Story Report for these fields now. Please let me know when this enhancement is planned.

entry.title: Higher Duty Assignment should be made primary for AD integration
entry.content:
For SF to AD integration, if an employee have multiple profiles with the standard profile, and multiple Higher duty profiles, whenever a Higher duty profile is active, then the higher duty profile should be taken as a primary profile instead of the standard profile. 

Any suggestions to achieve this

entry.title: Inquiry about reading nationalId from SuccessFactors and updating it in Cloud Identity Services
entry.content: I want to read the nationalId from SuccessFactors using Identity Provisioning Service and update it in the Login name (UserName) field in Cloud Identity Services. In SuccessFactors, the nationalId is available under PerNationalId. In IPS, I added the attributes 'personKeyNav,PerNationalId/nationalId' to sf.user.attributes.expand and included 'PerNationalId' in sf.user.attributes. I am using the following source transformation in IPS: { "sourcePath": "perNationalIdNav/results[0]/nationalId", "targetPath": "$.urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User:" } And in the Cloud Identity Services transformation, I have: { "sourcePath": "$.urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User:", "targetPath": "$.userName", "correlationAttribute": true } I want to confirm if these configurations are correct for reading nationalId from SuccessFactors and updating it into the UserName field in Cloud Identity Services. I am requesting to open a support case for further assistance.

entry.title: Configuration SSO between SAC to S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am planning to establish Single Sign-On (SSO) between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and SAP S/4HANA, as well as between SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA (where SAP S/4HANA is deployed on a Private Cloud). Azure AD work as identity provider and SAP IAS work as proxy

I would appreciate your guidance on the overall setup, as I have a few questions and would like to clarify some points:

  1. We currently have multiple SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) and Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) tenants provisioned by SAP. For example:

    • Landscape A (Test): IAS (Ariba Applications) and IPS (Ariba Applications, BTP, BIZX)
    • Landscape B (Production): Similar bundles
    • Landscape C (Test): IAS (BTP, BIZX) and IPS (BTP, BIZX) and 2 more 

    My question is: If I create a new SAP BTP subaccount and subscribe to IAS, will a new IAS tenant be created automatically and any additional license count ? If I want to use one of the existing IAS tenants instead of creating a new one, what is the recommended approach during the subscription and configuration process?

  2. For establishing SSO between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and SAP S/4HANA, is it necessary to create a separate SAP BTP subaccount and subscribe to SAC within that subaccount, or can the existing BTP landscape be used?

I would appreciate any guidance or best practices regarding this architecture and configuration.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards Shahid

entry.title: Request to Enable Onboarding SSO with a Single Email Notification from Onboarding Email Services
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have received a business requirement to enable Single Sign-On (SSO) for candidates during the Onboarding process.

The expectation is that only one email notification should be triggered from Onboarding Email Services, and this email should include the SSO login link for the candidate. No additional or separate system-generated emails should be sent as part of the onboarding initiation process.

Could you please confirm:

  1. Whether this configuration is supported in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding.
  2. If supported, the required settings and configuration steps to ensure that only a single onboarding email is sent containing the SSO link.
  3. Any prerequisites, limitations, or best practices associated with this setup.
  4. Whether there are any known impacts on candidate access, authentication, or onboarding workflow communications.

We would appreciate your guidance on achieving this requirement and any relevant documentation or KBAs that can assist with the configuration.

Regards,

Swapna

entry.title: Clarification Needed: Label Printing Functionality in Public Cloud Without EWM Integration
entry.content:
Good day SAP team,

 

We would like to request your guidance regarding a functional requirement related to goods receipt and goods issue processes. Our business needs to generate and print labels during both inbound and outbound movements. We understand that, by design, this functionality is typically integrated with the EWM module.

However, in our case, EWM is not implemented, and therefore we do not have access to the standard labeling capabilities provided through that module.

Given this limitation, we would appreciate your advice on what must be configured or developed in SAP Public Cloud to enable label printing during goods movements. As you know, the Public Cloud version has more restricted development options, which makes it essential for us to understand the recommended approach or available alternatives within the allowed extensibility framework.

Any guidance, best practices, or official recommendations you can provide will be extremely valuable for defining the correct solution.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: E-way bill issue
entry.content: While generating the e-Way Bill, we are encountering an error. Please find below the document process for your reference and analysis.

entry.title: 1. No Pin code found for sender 2. No State Code found for sender
entry.content:
While generating the e-Way Bill, we are encountering an error. Please find below the document process for your reference and analysis.

Error : -

1. No Pin code found for sender

2. No State Code found for sender

entry.title: User not as Member of Role after provisioning IAS to AS ABAP
entry.content:
Hi team,

 

we are currently testing user provisioning to AS ABAP following Docu: SAP Application Server ABAP | SAP Help Portal 

The connection succeeded and we were able to update user Attributes for a testuser.

We are testing the Role assignment with Group "ZS_SR_X_X_0257" wich is representative in CIS and our AS ABAP.

The testuser is assigned to this group in CIS, but wont get assigned in AS ABAP after a read or sync Job. 

The technical user has SAP_ALL so it wont be authorization issues. We can also see that the user provisioning is deleting all users wich were already assigned to that group in AS ABAP when we set parameter "ips.overwrite.existedbefore.assignments =true". So the groups is detected and can be edited.

 

Regarding to target system transformation (default from Docu) the groups are set as following:

{
                "sourcePath": "$.members[*].value",
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.USERLIST[?(@.USERNAME)]"
            }

In the provisioning log we can see that the system is updating the correct role "ZS_SR_X_X_0257" and user "XY"

"group:ZS_SR_X_X_0257,
system=SC2,
content={"ROLE_NAME":"ZS_SR_X_X_0257","USERLIST":[{"USERNAME":"XY"}]}"

Greetings
Rouven

entry.title: IPS for CALM
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I want to provision users from IAS to Cloud ALM/BTP using IPS (IAS->CloudALM)

Do I need to activate a Cloud Foundry environment in my SAP BTP subaccount for this user provisioning scenario ?

If Cloud Foundry is required, is it free of charge for this scenario?

Thanks in advance for your feedback.

Best Regards

entry.title: IAS to SAP S/4 (On-Premise) user sync issue
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to setup user sync from IAS to our on-prem SAP S/4 system to manage user ids in real time (as they are created in IAS or removed from IAS).

We have followed steps form documentation below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/btpcfsec/3519330578.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/target-sap-s-4hana-on-premise

However, when we are running the sync, we are getting below error:

OnPremiseConnectivityConfiguration is not found

error=Unable to write entity to target system with name DS4CLNT300 Caused by: OnPremiseConnectivityConfiguration is not found

We didn't find any documentation related to this sync error and hence are raising this ticket to get information as to what config might be missing in this case.

entry.title: Assignment of Scoped roles in Datasphere via cloud identity services
entry.content:
Hi,
Is it possible to assign custom Scoped roles in SAP Datasphere via Cloud identity services- Identity authentication as source and SAP Datasphere as Target system?

entry.title: Migrating from Older BTP version of IAS to Latest Cloud Identity services SCIM v2.0
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

Request you to please provide KBA or help with the steps of, on how to migrate to Cloud Identity services Latest version of IAS from the Previous BTP version of IAS. Attached is the deprecation email we received. Also, can you please let me know, where can I find the BTP links my instance. We have lost it some where.

Kindest Regards,

Santosh

entry.title: Request for Activation URL for Email Template
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,
We are updating the account activation email template and need the activation URL that is referenced by the following tag:

<a href="${user.activate_account_link}">${user.activate_account_link}</a> 

Could you please provide:The actual URL generated by ${user.activate_account_link}.Any prerequisites or configuration required for this variable to populate correctly.Confirmation that this URL can be embedded directly into our email template for account activation.This information is required to ensure the activation link is rendered correctly in the email notification.

entry.title: ValidTo field sync from SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

we need to auto populate field Valid To in IPS. We'd like to understand how this field works and how to auto populate it with e.g. Exit Date or custom field (custom01) from SuccessFactors. 

We're currently trying to set it up for test environment, but eventually it needs to be configured for PROD SF and IAS instances. 

I first tried to populate Valid To field with a fixed value just to check correctness of the transformation but unfortunately it does not work. Please see my target transformation below and advise what is incorrect: 

{
                "constant": "2026-12-01T00:00:00Z",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['validTo']"
            },

We're using for Source API version 2 and version 1 for Target.

Thank you in advance,

Aleksandra

entry.title: Question regarding IAS Group Filtering and Authorization Mapping
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a question regarding the runtime processing of group attributes in SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS).

In our landscape, users are authenticated by Microsoft Entra ID, and many users belong to a large number of Entra groups that are used for different enterprise applications.

For some SAP applications, authorization is evaluated locally after authentication. However, for other SAP applications, authorization is based on mapping Microsoft Entra groups to application roles or SAP role collections. In these scenarios, the groups received in the SAML assertion (or other supported token) determine the user's access within the application.

Our question is:

If Microsoft Entra ID sends a multi-valued groups claim containing many group memberships (for example, 50 groups), can SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) filter or transform this attribute on a per-application basis so that only the groups relevant to the target application are included in the outgoing SAML assertion?

For example:

  • Microsoft Entra user belongs to 50 groups.
  • Signavio requires only 2 of those groups.
  • SAP Build Work Zone requires a different subset.
  • Another SAP application requires yet another subset.

Can IAS be configured so that each application receives only its relevant groups, without exposing unrelated Entra groups?

We are asking this because we would like IAS to act as the central identity and authorization abstraction layer for SAP applications. Our objective is to:

  • Minimize the number of groups sent to each application.
  • Avoid exposing unrelated enterprise groups to applications.
  • Keep SAML assertions small and application-specific.
  • Centralize authorization mapping within IAS instead of requiring application-specific group filtering.

If IAS supports this capability, could you please advise which feature or configuration should be used (for example, application-specific attribute mappings, conditional transformations, or another recommended approach)?
